item.title: Inflammatory bowel disease outcomes among hospitalized homeless individuals.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - There is a paucity of outcome data among homeless individuals with inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD). We reported rates of homelessness among inpatients with Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative col...

item.title: HLA-DRB1*01:03 in patients with inflammatory bowel disease: a genotype-phenotype association study.
item.description: The lancet. Gastroenterology & hepatology - The association between HLA-DRB1*01:03 and severe ulcerative colitis has long been recognised. However, the recent discovery of an association with neutralising auto-antibodies against IL-10 raises...

item.title: Perioperative risk factors for disease recurrence following ileocecal resection in Crohn's disease: implications for early immunosuppressive therapy.
item.description: Updates in surgery - Crohn's disease is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) affecting 1.85-10.5 individuals per 100,000 per year. Approximately 30-50% of patients still require surgery during their lifetime. Whi...

item.title: Utilization of indocyanine green fluorescence angiography in redo IPAA surgery.
item.description: International journal of colorectal disease - Redo/revisional ileal pouch-anal anastomosis (IPAA) surgery is technically challenging and more likely to require mesenteric lengthening maneuvers, largely due to mesenteric reach issues, which may...

item.title: Cystic Fibrosis-Associated Inflammatory Bowel Disease: a Case Series and Literature Review.
item.description: Clinics and research in hepatology and gastroenterology - The management of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) in patients with cystic fibrosis (CF) at risk of respiratory failure and severe lung infection is poorly described. The objective of this study wa...

item.title: Risk Factors for Small Bowel Crohn's Disease Recurrence after Total Proctocolectomy.
item.description: Diseases of the colon and rectum - Surgical management of Crohn's disease of the colon and rectum frequently requires total proctocolectomy with end ileostomy. There is limited data on the need for Crohn's disease-directed therapy a...

item.title: Using clinical factors to predict treatment outcomes and health-care utilization in Crohn's disease patients initiating advanced therapies.
item.description: Clinics and research in hepatology and gastroenterology - Crohn's disease (CD) prevalence is rising globally. Early optimal therapy is key to achieving remission. A Clinical Decision Support Tool (CDST) predicts outcomes for CD patients on vedolizumab, th...

item.title: Endoscopic Management of Pouch Volvulus as a Complication of Ileal Pouch Anal Anastomosis in Patients With Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - Pouch volvulus is a rare but potentially morbid complication after total proctocolectomy with ileal pouch anal anastomosis, typically managed surgically. Evidence for definitive endoscopic therapy ...

item.title: Not Just a Cyst: A Draining Gluteal Lesion Revealing a Rectocutaneous Fistula in Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - We report a 48-year-old man with poorly controlled ulcerative colitis (UC) who presented with severe rectal pain, hematochezia, and a chronically draining lesion on the left buttock. Initial cross-...

item.title: Endoscopic Ultrasound-Guided Drainage of an Intra-Abdominal Abscess in a Patient with Crohn's Disease.

item.title: Impact of Crohn's perianal fistulas on healthcare resource utilization: a US chart review study.
item.description: Journal of medical economics - Multicenter study - Perianal fistulas are a common complication of Crohn's disease. Despite the current treatment options, fistula recurrence/treatment failure rates are high. The aim of this study was to assess healt...

item.title: Advanced phenotypes and favorable 1-year outcomes with accelerated step-up strategy in newly diagnosed inflammatory bowel disease in a tuberculosis-endemic, resource-constrained setting.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - The incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is rising across Southeast Asia, yet real-world data on clinical phenotypes, treatment strategies, and outcomes in tuberculosis (TB)-endemic, resou...

item.title: Clinical significance of isolated periappendiceal red patch in ulcerative colitis: A systematic review, meta-analysis, and meta-regression.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is characterized by continuous mucosal inflammation beginning in the rectum with variable proximal extension. However, up to 17% of patients demonstrate patchy cecal inflamm...

item.title: Beyond intestinal failure: Expanding therapeutic frontiers of glucagon-like peptide-2 in gastrointestinal disease.
item.description: World journal of gastrointestinal pharmacology and therapeutics - Review - Short bowel syndrome represents a severe form of intestinal failure characterized by malabsorption and dependence on parenteral nutrition (PN). Advances in gut hormone research have positioned gluc...

item.title: Malleolar Stress Fracture as the First Manifestation of Undiagnosed Crohn's Disease: A Case Report.
item.description: Journal of orthopaedic case reports - Case report - Stress fractures (SFs) typically result from repetitive high-impact activities in athletes or military personnel. Their occurrence in young, non-athletic individuals without significant mechanical ...

item.title: Surgical interventions in inflammatory bowel disease: Indications, techniques, outcomes, and future directions.
item.description: World journal of gastrointestinal pharmacology and therapeutics - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), encompassing ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD), is a chronic relapsing condition in which surgical intervention remains a cornerstone of management ...

item.title: Short- and long-term outcomes of IPAA for ulcerative colitis in Latin America: A retrospective, multicentre assessment.
item.description: Colorectal disease : the official journal of the Association of Coloproctology of Great Britain and Ireland - Multicenter study - Data on ileal pouch-anal anastomosis (IPAA) outcomes in ulcerative colitis (UC) patients from resource-constrained settings, such as Latin America, are limited. This study aimed to evaluate demogra...

item.title: Distinct Serum and Tissue Markers Predict Fibrosis in Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Cells - Fibrosis in Crohn's Disease (CD) occurs when there is continuous inflammation and repair mechanisms, which may lead to fibrotic strictures and ultimately intestinal surgical resection. There are li...

item.title: Differential Expression Profiles of Orphan Nuclear Receptors (NR4A) and N-myc Downstream-Regulated Gene Family (NDRG) in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - NDRG1 protein engages with the orphan nuclear receptor NR4A1, effectively suppressing the transcriptional activity of NF-κB and influencing the inflammatory response. However, the specific roles of...
